bugs.error_message: User asked for no commits in the graph, not a revert or re-rooted branch.
bugs.root_cause: I initially stopped at a new root commit, which still left a commit in the local graph.
bugs.fix: Converted main into an unborn branch and deleted local remote-tracking refs so no commits remain reachable from local refs.

bugs.root_cause: Function uses await but wasn't declared async
bugs.fix: Added async modifier | Also: payload = alert.model_dump(mode="json"); database.save_alert(payload) | Also: # ---- realtime push ----; def _broadcast(alert: dict) -> None:

bugs.root_cause: No early return/throw for edge case: process.env.CHROME_PATH

bugs.error_message: npm install in whatsapp_bridge fails: 'Failed to set up chrome v146! ... All providers failed: read ECONNRESET' (Puppeteer postinstall Chromium download), exit code 1; rollback then hits EPERM rmdir on node_modules.
bugs.root_cause: whatsapp-web.js depends on puppeteer, whose postinstall downloads a ~150MB Chromium. The download was killed by a flaky network (ECONNRESET) and the whole install failed.
bugs.fix: Install with PUPPETEER_SKIP_DOWNLOAD=true (bash) / $env:PUPPETEER_SKIP_DOWNLOAD='true' (PowerShell), then drive the system browser: index.js auto-detects Chrome/Edge (or CHROME_PATH) and passes it as puppeteer.executablePath. Verified: bridge launches Chrome and renders the linked-device QR.

bugs.error_message: WebSocket /ws/alerts rejected with HTTP 403 (websockets InvalidStatus) even though the route was added to main.py.
bugs.root_cause: uvicorn --reload (WatchFiles) on Windows stalled: it logged 'Reloading...' but the new worker never started, so the old worker (without the new /ws route) kept serving. Starlette returns 403 for a WebSocket path that matches no registered route, which looked like a code bug but was stale code.
bugs.fix: Stopped the server and restarted WITHOUT --reload (run fresh on each code change). WS push then verified end-to-end: connect -> POST /ingest toxic -> alert pushed over the socket. Lesson: on Windows, prefer a manual restart over --reload for this project.
